summary: Matched splGetSplinePoint by refactoring spline interpolation code into named helper functions for Bezier, B-spline, and cardinal spline point evaluation, and by replacing anonymous HSD_Spline field names with semantic names. The PR also propagated those field names into arc-length parameter code. Evidence is limited to the diff; no review comments or PR body details were provided.
what_changed: Removed two static inline helper bodies, splGetSplinePoint_inline and splGetSplinePoint_inline2, and rewrote splGetSplinePoint directly around a switch on spline->type.
Added exported spline helper functions: splGetCardinalPoint(Vec3*, Vec3*, f32, f32), splGetBSplinePoint(Vec3*, Vec3*, f32), and splGetBezierPoint(Vec3*, Vec3*, f32).
Moved Bezier basis computation into splGetBezierPoint using bez0, bez1, bez2, and bez3 coefficient names.
Moved B-spline basis computation into splGetBSplinePoint using k1_6 and b0 through b3 coefficient names.
Moved cardinal spline basis computation into splGetCardinalPoint using tension, u2, u3, and car0 through car3 coefficient names.
Reworked splGetSplinePoint argument names from arg1/arg2 to spline/u and local names such as cp and idx.
Kept special handling for u == 1.0F, including endpoint behavior for linear and Bezier cases, B-spline evaluation at 1.0f using &spline->cv[idx] - 1, and cardinal endpoint assignment through cp[1].
Renamed HSD_Spline fields in spline.h from x0, x2, x4, x8, xC, segments, and coeffs to type, numcv, tension, cv, totalLength, segLength, and segPoly.
Removed the visible x1 byte field from HSD_Spline, preserving offsets by relying on padding between the u8 type field at +0 and s16 numcv at +2.
Updated splArcLengthGetParameter and spl_GetCoeffs to use the renamed HSD_Spline fields: segLength, segPoly, totalLength, type, and numcv.
smart_moves: Factoring each spline basis into its own named function made the switch cases in splGetSplinePoint simpler and likely helped the compiler produce the desired function boundaries and instruction ordering for a 100% match.
Using semantic struct field names clarified the meaning of previously anonymous fields without changing the documented offsets.
The refactor reused the same Vec3 coefficient application pattern across Bezier, B-spline, and cardinal splines, making formulas easier to compare against expected math.
The endpoint branch for u == 1.0F was kept separate from the general u < 1.0F path, preserving type-specific final-control-point behavior rather than forcing all cases through the generic path.
Renaming spline control-point pointers to cp and interpolation parameter to u aligned local variable names with common spline terminology.
decomp_lessons: For matching decompilation, extracting duplicated interpolation math into plausible library-style helpers can be more effective than keeping large static inline switch bodies.
When a struct has unknown padding, it can be better to omit a dummy field if natural alignment preserves offsets; here type at +0 and numcv at +2 imply a padding byte at +1.
Semantic struct fields should be propagated consistently through all users in the same subsystem to avoid mixing x-style names with meaningful names.
Endpoint behavior in spline code can differ by spline type; do not assume u == 1.0F can share the same path as fractional segment interpolation.
Small expression ordering details, such as 4.0F + (3.0F * u3 - 6.0F * u2) in the B-spline basis and explicit casts around idx subtraction, can matter for matching.
naming_conventions: Use cp for a Vec3 control-point pointer.
Use u for normalized spline interpolation parameter.
Use basis coefficient prefixes matching spline kind: bez0..bez3, b0..b3, car0..car3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced generic inline helper calls with explicit named functions for Bezier, B-spline, and cardinal basis evaluation.
Kept polynomial temporaries in a matching-friendly sequence: u2, u3, u_1 or u_12, then basis coefficients.
Preserved separate switch handling for u < 1.0F versus u == 1.0F rather than trying to collapse endpoint cases.
Used direct struct field renames while preserving offsets in HSD_Spline.
Used `(f32) idx` in `t = t - (f32) idx` after assigning `idx = t`, which may reflect the required conversion sequence for matching.
Removed `PAD_STACK(8)` from splGetSplinePoint in the rewritten version, suggesting the new structure matched without artificial stack padding.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or document HSD_Spline fields beyond type, numcv, tension, cv, totalLength, segLength, and segPoly.
Search for uses of splGetBezierPoint, splGetBSplinePoint, and splGetCardinalPoint to confirm whether they are expected exported baselib symbols.
Compare this PR with later spline matching work involving splArcLengthPoint or Simpson's rule integration.
Check whether other HSD structs can remove explicit padding fields when natural alignment preserves offsets.
